summary: Crisp soundboard capture of a solid ’91 show highlighted by a smoking Black Peter and stellar Sugar Magnolia, with dark‑moody drums/space.
review: The Miller SBD source delivers an exceptionally clear and dynamic listening experience – the first few seconds of the outdoor crowd bleed fade seamlessly into full soundboard fidelity.  Musically the concert is a strong example of the band’s ’91 form, featuring standout moments like Black Peter, Sugar Magnolia, the Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain segue and an especially dark, moody Drums/Space stretch.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers praise both the recording clarity and the musical performance; only one outlier low rating exists, so overall quality is judged very good to excellent.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Initial ~15 s outdoor audience capture then seamless transition to full SBD; reviewers describe the sound as crisp, dynamic and ‘world‑class’ for a Miller recording.

standout_songs: Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain
Playing in the Band > Uncle John's Band
show_quality.setlist_flow: Opening with an unexpected Touch set a quirky tone; the second set builds with a powerful pre‑space segment and climaxes in a moody, dark Drums/Space that many reviewers highlighted as exceptional.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s playing is praised overall; one reviewer notes he sounds best when he ‘sounds like Garcia’, especially during the space jam.
band_member_comments.Keys: Bruce Hornsby’s accordion/piano work divided opinion – some prefer his piano over accordion; Vince Welnick is described as “actually OK”.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drums and ensuing space are repeatedly called dark, moody and exceptionally good.
